title: AdapterResult
description: The four-axis result an agent-CLI adapter MUST return from invoke(mode, prompt, model, session, timeout, env). See docs/pipeline/adapter-spec.md (spec_version 1). A flat failure enum is explicitly NON-conformant: every result MUST carry all four orthogonal axes (process, provider, verdict, voteEligibility) so no information the aggregator depends on (INV-40 / INV-48) is lost.

spec_version.description: The adapter-spec version this result conforms to. MUST be 1 for this revision.

adapter.description: The agent-CLI this adapter wraps (the AGENT_CMD value).

mode.description: The structural invocation mode. Modes differ structurally, not cosmetically (e.g. codex dev = `codex exec --json` + resume; codex review = `codex review` from a PR worktree, no resume).

process.description: Process-liveness axis: how the CLI process exited.

rc.description: The CLI exit code. 0=clean, 124=coreutils `timeout` TERM expiry, 137=SIGKILL (--kill-after escalation), 143=SIGTERM (dispatcher-induced), 70=fail-closed no-worktree sentinel (codex review, INV-62).

signal.description: The terminating signal name if the process was signalled (e.g. SIGTERM, SIGKILL), else null.

timedOut.description: true iff the process was killed by the per-side wall-clock cap (AGENT_REVIEW_TIMEOUT / dev timeout). Maps from rc 124/137 (INV-48).

provider.description: Provider-classification axis: why the model backend failed, with evidence, when it did. `none` means the provider did not fail (the CLI ran the model successfully regardless of verdict outcome).

class.description: none=provider OK; quota=429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (INV-58); auth=login/OAuth/device-flow failure (INV-58 agy, INV-61 kiro); config=structural prep failure that an operator must fix (codex no-worktree INV-62, bad model id, missing trust flag); transient=retryable upstream blip (codex stream-error INV-59/INV-62 5xx reconnect).

evidence.description: Human-readable, sanitized reason string surfaced to the operator (e.g. "quota-exhausted (Antigravity 429: daily quota reached; resets in 33h48m45s)"). REQUIRED when class != none.

resetHint.description: Recovery window when the provider published one (e.g. "Resets in 33h48m45s"), else null. Only meaningful for class=quota.

verdict.description: Verdict-presence axis: whether the adapter produced a classifiable verdict artifact within the poll window. When state=valid, payloadRef MUST be a non-empty string (enforced by the top-level allOf): a 'valid' verdict the wrapper/aggregator cannot locate is non-conformant.

state.description: valid=a well-formed verdict artifact was produced (payloadRef REQUIRED, non-empty); absent=no verdict artifact within the window (drives drop/timeout-veto on the voteEligibility axis); malformed=an artifact was produced but failed schema validation (fail-safe: treated as absent, never as a silent PASS).

payloadRef.description: Path/URL/comment-id of the verdict artifact (which MUST satisfy verdict-artifact.schema.json). REQUIRED and non-empty when state=valid; null/absent otherwise.

voteEligibility.description: Aggregation axis: how this result participates in the INV-40 unanimous-PASS vote.

state.description: pass=deciding PASS; fail=deciding FAIL (substantive); drop=removed from the vote (unavailable, e.g. rc 0 + no verdict); timeout-veto=deciding FAIL that vetoes the merge (rc 124/137 + no verdict, INV-48); not-applicable=this mode does not vote (dev-new/dev-resume/e2e-browser).

reason.description: Optional short token explaining a non-pass eligibility (e.g. "unavailable", "timed-out", "substantive").

allOf.description: Clause P1 + the timeout-veto worked example: a process killed by the wall-clock cap (rc 124 = `timeout` TERM-expiry, or 137 = `--kill-after` SIGKILL) that produced NO verdict (verdict.state absent|malformed) MUST set process.timedOut=true AND resolve voteEligibility.state=timeout-veto (a deciding FAIL, INV-48) — it MUST NOT validate as drop/unavailable. A timed-out run that DID post a verdict (verdict.state=valid) is exempt: the matched verdict wins (INV-40), so this conditional is gated on the no-verdict case only.

A verdict.state=valid MUST carry a non-empty payloadRef (a string >=1 char): a 'valid' verdict the wrapper/aggregator cannot locate is non-conformant. payloadRef stays optional/nullable for absent|malformed.
